title: Will any U.S. House member visit Iran before Jul 1, 2026?

current.lean_note: ADVERSARIAL VETO: The position is overconfident and lacks sufficient evidence to justify the proposed probability. The divergence from the market is not supported by credible reasoning, and the edge is not real due to the lack of actionable information or ev
adversarial_concerns: The proposed probability (0.15) is significantly higher than the market-implied probability (0.0025), indicating a large divergence that is not justified by the evidence provided.

The reasoning relies on vague claims about 'dynamic international relations' and 'unreported diplomatic efforts' without specific, credible sources or evidence to support the claim that a visit is likely.
The forecaster assumes a level of confidence (high) that is not substantiated by the thin and speculative nature of the evidence.
current.rationale_summary: Historically, U.S. House members have rarely visited Iran due to the politically sensitive nature of such trips. No specific evidence of planned visits between 2025 and 2026 has been found in the consulted sources. However, the possibility cannot be ruled out entirely due to the dynamic nature of international relations and potential unreported diplomatic efforts.
key_drivers: No direct evidence of planned visits
reference_classes: Diplomatic visits by U.S. legislators to politically sensitive countries
Legislative travel patterns involving Middle Eastern nations

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 2%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: No sitting US House member has visited Iran in the modern era; with an active conflict, a Level 4 advisory, and no planned CODEL, a visit before July 1 is virtually impossible (marginally above Rubio as an informal trip is less institutionally constrained).
reference_classes: 0 known sitting House-member visits to Iran

history.lean_note: I lean NO (my prob 3%) but it isn't +EV at the ask — no value bet (betting the other side would contradict my forecast).
history.rationale_summary: All US-Iran engagement is in neutral venues (Geneva/Switzerland/Rome), not Tehran; no House member traveling to Iran; physically near-unprecedented. Corrected down from local overestimate.
key_drivers: talks in neutral venues not Tehran
reference_classes: US officials physically visiting Iran post-1979

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 2%, modal NO) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: No US House member has visited Iran since 1979 and none is reported planning one; the slight bump only reflects the 435-member pool and unannounced-visit tail risk.
reference_classes: House member trips to Iran since 1979 ~ zero
